Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Laboratory results are largely within normal limits. Notable findings include mildly elevated chloride, erythrocyte count, platelet mean volume, and platelet distribution width, which may reflect mild dehydration or reactive changes but are not alarming in isolation. Renal, glycemic, and lipid profiles are unremarkable. No evidence of acute pathology. Correlate with clinical context if symptoms are present.
